sanskrit:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
transliteration:
aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarva-bhūtāśaya-sthitaḥ |
aham ādiś ca madhyaṁ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca ||
literal_meaning: I am the Self, O Arjuna, seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all entities.
contextual_meaning: Krishna affirms that the same awareness animating every creature is the singular Self—timeless, centerless, and boundaryless—dissolving the illusion of separateness that fuels anxiety and identity diffusion.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth; shift from egoic narrative to transpersonal identification, reducing existential isolation.
trigger_scenario: A first responder, after a near-death experience on duty, senses an unbroken presence connecting every victim and rescuer, realizing the same awareness looks through all eyes.
anchor_text: The same awareness lives in everyone; feel it and fear dissolves.
